name: Header
description: Composable header with slots for brand, navigation, search, and actions. Supports dropdown nav groups via Header.NavMenu. Designed for use within AppShell with responsive mobile support.

icons.description: Optional icon overrides for Header.Trigger (menu/close) and Header.NavMenu chevron

when: Primary site or app header inside AppShell
Header with responsive mobile menu trigger
Grouping related nav items under a dropdown menu
guidelines: Use Header.SkipLink for accessibility (skip to main content)
In default layout: include Header.Brand for logo
In sidebar layout: omit Header.Brand (logo in sidebar)
Header.Trigger integrates with SidebarProvider for mobile menus
Header.Trigger composes your onClick handler with sidebar toggle behavior
Header.Nav is hidden on mobile; use sidebar for mobile navigation
Use Header.Spacer to push items apart
Use Header.NavMenu to group related nav items under a dropdown
Use Header.NavMenuItem inside Header.NavMenu for dropdown items
Header sub-components forward DOM props (id, aria-*, data-*, handlers) to their rendered elements
Header.NavItem asChild composes click handlers instead of overwriting the child handler
Use the Header icons prop to replace internal mobile trigger and dropdown chevron icons with any icon package
For rich dropdown content (titles, descriptions, icons), use NavigationMenu instead of Header.Nav
NavigationMenu also provides an automatic mobile drawer, replacing Header.Trigger + Sidebar for mobile nav
accessibility: Navigation has aria-label for screen readers
NavMenu dropdown opens with click and is keyboard navigable

examples.name: For Default Layout
examples.description: Header with brand, nav, and actions. Use with AppShell layout="default".
examples.code:
<ThemeProvider defaultMode="light">
  <Header>
    <Header.SkipLink />
    <Header.Trigger />
    <Header.Brand href="/">MyApp</Header.Brand>
    <Header.Nav>
      <Header.NavItem href="/dashboard" active>Dashboard</Header.NavItem>
      <Header.NavItem href="/projects">Projects</Header.NavItem>
      <Header.NavItem href="/settings">Settings</Header.NavItem>
    </Header.Nav>
    <Header.Spacer />
    <Header.Actions>
      <ThemeToggle size="md" />
      <Button variant="secondary" size="sm">Sign In</Button>
    </Header.Actions>
  </Header>
</ThemeProvider>

examples.name: With Dropdown Nav
examples.description: Header with a dropdown menu grouping related navigation links.
examples.code:
<ThemeProvider defaultMode="light">
  <Header>
    <Header.Brand href="/">MyApp</Header.Brand>
    <Header.Nav>
      <Header.NavItem href="/components" active>Components</Header.NavItem>
      <Header.NavItem href="/blocks">Blocks</Header.NavItem>
      <Header.NavMenu label="Docs">
        <Header.NavMenuItem href="/getting-started">Getting Started</Header.NavMenuItem>
        <Header.NavMenuItem href="/cli">CLI Reference</Header.NavMenuItem>
        <Header.NavMenuItem href="/mcp">MCP Tools</Header.NavMenuItem>
      </Header.NavMenu>
      <Header.NavItem href="/blog">Blog</Header.NavItem>
    </Header.Nav>
    <Header.Spacer />
    <Header.Actions>
      <ThemeToggle size="md" />
    </Header.Actions>
  </Header>
</ThemeProvider>

examples.name: For Sidebar Layout
examples.description: Header without brand (logo is in sidebar). Use with AppShell layout="sidebar" or "sidebar-floating".

examples.name: Minimal
examples.description: Clean header with just trigger and actions

examples.name: With Search
examples.description: Header featuring a prominent search input
examples.code:
Docs
Search documentation...

examples.name: With Skip Link
examples.description: Accessible header with skip link for keyboard navigation
examples.code:
<Header>
  <Header.SkipLink href="#main-content">Skip to content</Header.SkipLink>
  <Header.Brand>Accessible App</Header.Brand>
  <Header.Nav>
    <Header.NavItem href="/" active>Home</Header.NavItem>
    <Header.NavItem href="/about">About</Header.NavItem>
  </Header.Nav>
</Header>

examples.name: With NavigationMenu
examples.description: Header using NavigationMenu for rich dropdown content panels with titles, descriptions, and automatic mobile drawer.
examples.code:
MyApp Products
Pricing

relations.note: Header is typically used inside AppShell.Header
ThemeToggle is commonly placed in Header.Actions
Use NavigationMenu inside Header for rich dropdown nav with auto mobile drawer

propsSummary: icons: { menu?, close?, navMenuChevron? } - custom internal control icons (ReactNode or render function)
Header.Trigger composes click handler with Sidebar toggle behavior
Header.NavMenu provides dropdown groups via Base UI Menu
